title: Ucraina  – supraviețuire prin europenizare
summary: The article argues that Ukraine's Europeanization has evolved from a diplomatic project into an existential survival mechanism against Russian imperial revisionism. It traces Ukraine's European trajectory from the Orange Revolution through the 2022 invasion, showing how war transformed EU integration from a policy goal into a component of national resilience, with over 170 billion euros mobilized by the EU between 2022-2025 and public support for EU membership reaching 85.5%.
talking_points: Ukraine's EU integration is not an emotional wartime reaction but a historical return to a cultural and political space it has always partially belonged to
The war eliminated strategic ambiguity and made multi-vector foreign policy impossible, turning Europeanization into a national survival mechanism
EU reforms—independent judiciary, anti-corruption, civil control of the military—now function as elements of wartime resilience rather than mere accession conditions
The EU has undergone a paradigm shift, mobilizing over 170 billion euros and treating Ukraine as a future member state whose stability is a European public good
EU enlargement can no longer be treated as a purely technical-bureaucratic process; it is now inseparable from European security policy
author: Nicolae Țîbrigan
